title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death stands a library filled with the lives we might have lived. Nora Seed explores those possibilities and discovers what makes a life worth choosing.

title: The Hobbit
author: J. R. R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins leaves his quiet home for an unexpected journey involving dwarves, a dragon, treasure, and the courage he never knew he had.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young shepherd follows a recurring dream across the desert and learns about purpose, sacrifice, and listening to the language of the world.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut wakes with no memory and must solve an impossible scientific mystery before humanity runs out of time.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A practical guide to writing readable, maintainable software through disciplined naming, functions, testing, and thoughtful refactoring.

title: Eloquent JavaScript
author: Marijn Haverbeke
description: A modern introduction to JavaScript that combines language fundamentals, browser programming, and hands-on projects.

title: You Don't Know JS Yet
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A focused exploration of JavaScript's core mechanisms, helping developers understand scope, closures, objects, and the language model in depth.

title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
author: Stuart Russell & Peter Norvig
description: A comprehensive introduction to intelligent agents, search, learning, reasoning, robotics, and the foundations of modern artificial intelligence.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An accessible journey through cosmology, black holes, time, and the enduring search for a unified understanding of the universe.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A sweeping account of the universe, scientific discovery, and humanity's place in a vast and ancient cosmos.

title: The Gene
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A history of genetics that connects scientific breakthroughs with the personal, ethical, and social questions surrounding heredity.

title: The Body
author: Bill Bryson
description: A lively tour of the human body, explaining how its systems work, fail, heal, and continue to surprise medical science.
